content.problemStatement:
The C++ Standard Template Library (STL) algorithm library provides a collection of functions specifically designed to operate on ranges of elements. These algorithms are highly optimized and can be used to perform a variety of operations on data structures like arrays, vectors, lists, and more. Understanding and utilizing the STL algorithm library effectively can greatly simplify coding tasks and improve the efficiency of your programs.

Here's a more detailed overview of the STL algorithm library:

**Key Characteristics:**

_Generic Programming:_ STL algorithms are designed to be generic, meaning they can work with a wide variety of data types, including user-defined types, as long as the necessary operations are supported (e.g., comparison, assignment).

_Iterator-Based:_ STL algorithms operate on ranges of elements defined by iterators. This makes them highly flexible and applicable to various data structures.

_Efficiency:_ The algorithms in the STL are implemented with efficiency in mind, often utilizing optimized algorithms and techniques to achieve high performance.


The algorithms in the C++ Standard Template Library (STL) are provided by the `` header. This header contains the declarations and definitions of various templated functions that implement common algorithms for working with collections of elements.
